predictions.title: When will SpaceX IPO?
predictions.reasoning: Base rate: Major private companies IPO ~5-10% per year. Updates: Musk has repeatedly stated SpaceX won't IPO until Mars missions are self-sustaining, likely 2030s. Company is cash-flow positive from Starlink, reducing IPO pressure. Timeline to 2026 is very short. Market price of 1% seems roughly accurate.

predictions.title: When will Klarna IPO?
predictions.reasoning: Base rate: Fintech unicorns IPO ~20-30% within 2-3 years when market conditions allow. Updates: Klarna has been preparing for IPO since 2021, hired IPO-ready executives, improved profitability. However, market closes Sept 2025, giving only ~8 months. 96% seems too high given execution risks and market volatility.

predictions.title: Who will the next Pope be?
predictions.reasoning: Base rate: Spanish cardinals have been ~5-8% of papal candidates historically. Updates: Current Pope Francis is 88 and healthy. Spain has several prominent cardinals but Italy/Vatican traditionally favored. Geographic diversity trend might help Spain. Market closes May 2025, requiring papal transition soon. 1% seems too low given base rates.

predictions.title: Will Robert F. Kennedy Jr. be the first person to leave the Trump Cabinet?
predictions.reasoning: Base rate: Cabinet members leave ~20-30% in first year, but 'first to leave' is much lower (~5-10%). Updates: RFK Jr. is controversial pick for HHS, faces Senate confirmation challenges, has history of clashing with establishment. However, Trump values loyalty and RFK brings unique coalition. 1% seems too low for such a polarizing figure.

predictions.title: Who will the next Pope be?
predictions.reasoning: Base rate: Italian cardinals historically ~30-40% of serious papal candidates, though declining. Updates: Vatican still Italy-based, Italian cardinals well-positioned institutionally. However, trend toward geographic diversity since Vatican II. Same timeline constraint as Spanish market. 1% significantly undervalues Italian historical advantage.

predictions.title: When will there be a half-trillionaire?

Updates: (+) AI/tech boom continuing, (+) asset inflation, (+) 11 months timeline reasonable for 2x wealth growth in bull market. (-) Market crashes could delay. Steelman opposite: Major recession/regulation. Market price seems reasonable given momentum.

(-) BTC is closer to ATH, (-) BTC typically leads crypto cycles, (-) very short timeline (weeks). Market underpricing ETH's volatility potential.

OpenAI has strong legal resources and the nonprofit-to-capped profit transition was documented. Market at 6% seems too low given some validity to claims.
